reviews.title: Punishing prudence – not the member-focused society they claim to be
reviews.body: I approached Market Harborough with what any reasonable borrower would assume to be a simple, responsible, and forward-looking request: a conversation about short-term flexibility on a mortgage during a predictable, temporary lull in rental income. No arrears. No crisis. Just prudence - the kind of early engagement that every lender, regulator, and financial-wellbeing guide encourages.What I encountered instead was a masterclass in how not to handle customers who are trying to stay ahead of the curve.The initial silence was telling. Weeks passed with no acknowledgement, no holding reply, no sign that anyone was paying attention. For a building society that trades so heavily on mutuality, community values, and personal service, this felt like a curtain being pulled back to reveal a far more indifferent reality. When a reply finally arrived, it read like a template churned out after reluctantly dusting off the inbox: references to missing emails, apologies that rang hollow, and an invitation to resend everything as though the burden naturally sits with the customer rather than the institution.The substance that followed was even more disheartening. Rather than engaging with the perfectly reasonable idea that a short, well-defined period might warrant proportionate, preventative support, the focus fixed instantly on “affordability assessments”, “financial difficulty”, and the need for detailed income-and-expenditure forms. Not as part of a tailored conversation, but as a default barrier to even discussing options.In regulatory terms, what troubles me most is how this experience sits against the FCA’s own expectations of lenders. The FCA’s mortgage support guidance and Dear CEO communications on the cost of living make it clear that firms should offer prospective and tailored forbearance where customers indicate they are, or reasonably expect to be, in payment difficulty - including BEFORE any payment is actually missed – and that support should be appropriate to the customer’s individual circumstances rather than driven by rigid process. Layered on top of that, the Consumer Duty now requires firms to act to deliver good outcomes, avoid foreseeable harm and support customers in pursuing their financial objectives, not simply protect their own risk metrics. Against that backdrop, the slow response, the lack of meaningful engagement, and the default push into a full “hardship” framing with credit-file warnings - rather than a proportionate, constructive conversation about short-term flexibility – feels, in my view, out of step with both the letter and the spirit of what the FCA says good mortgage lenders should be doing.This, for me, is where the experience strayed furthest from the principles MHBS claims to uphold. A customer taking proactive steps to plan carefully should not be met with suspicion, bureaucracy, and an implication that only hardship cases deserve attention. The FCA’s own guidance encourages lenders to support borrowers before difficulties escalate, to tailor their approach to individual circumstances, and to use discretion where a customer’s situation is temporary, logical, and easily resolved. Yet the impression here was of a lender more concerned with protecting its internal process map than with exercising common sense or acting in a member’s best interests.The tone throughout was transactional, not mutual. Defensive, not supportive. A society that prides itself on doing things “the right way” should not make customers feel like an inconvenience for trying to engage early and responsibly. And it should certainly not treat every enquiry as though it must be funnelled into the same bureaucracy that applies to long-term, distressed borrowers.In short, this experience left me with the view that MHBS struggles to live up to the values it promotes. If you are looking for a lender that genuinely listens, applies proportionality, and engages constructively with members who act in good faith, you may find the gulf between their marketing and their behaviour uncomfortably wide.

reviews.title: Easy to use website.
reviews.body: Easy to use website.Helpful customer service.Good rates on their products.

reviews.title: Brilliant
reviews.body: Brilliant, no trouble at all with doing the online bond maturity. Instructions followed. Very happy indeed.

reviews.title: Online registration
reviews.body: I rang MHBS yesterday and got through to Rachel in customer services who very kindly helped me register online as there are no branches available near me. After finishing the registration process, she explained that I would have to wait until I receive my customer number to call back the following day. When I rang again today, I got through to Claire who was very helpful as well. Both the ladies are highly professional, friendly and very knowledgeable. They are a great asset to MHBS.

reviews.title: Helpful
reviews.body: Lorraine was very helpful and thorough with my mums complicated questions. We had tried to use the online service but it wouldn’t let us. Lorraine went through everything step by step until we were satisfied.

reviews.title: Account closure online
reviews.body: I wanted to close my account as there are no branches near me anymore. I spoke to Rachel at customer services and she was extremely helpful, patient and very efficient. She helped me go through the registration process online and I managed to close my account without having to visit a branch as the online closure process as very simple. Please convey my sincere thanks to Rachel. She is a true asset to MHBS.

reviews.title: Elvira was excellent
reviews.body: Elvira was excellent. Gave us sound advice and made opening our new accounts really straight forward.

reviews.title: Very Polite
reviews.body: Well, I had a wonderful experience at the Market Harborough Building Society. I came in with a list of questions, and they were all answered brilliantly. Polite and friendly staff. It good to talk to local people. Highly recommend.

reviews.title: I had a meeting this morning with Andy…
reviews.body: I had a meeting this morning with Andy at the Kettering branch to discuss my savings and how to get the best option for the future. Andy could not have been more helpful and explained the different types accounts open to me, all done with a smile and a coffee.I really like the fact I can talk face to face , you can’t beat the personal touch many thanks!

reviews.title: Visited MHBS needed advice was taken to…
reviews.body: Visited MHBS needed advice was taken to a private room , we were seen by lovely lady , unfortunately didn’t get her name because but was polite and friendly helped with advice to make the most of our funds , it’s a pleasure to be able to see a person face to face , with all the bank closures we don’t do online banking ,so we use MHBS thank you

reviews.body: Great staff and a very professional financial organisation. As a fairly new customer - really impressed!

reviews.title: generally very good
reviews.body: generally very good.  One suggestion - either a text or email when you receive a bank transfer and is credited to my. account.  Had to keep logging in.

reviews.title: £100kdue back today (Friday) Nothing…
reviews.body: £100k due back today (Friday) Nothing repaid. Very lucrative for MHBS to keep my funds over the weekend, and pay me nothing, Very cynical! Hopefully I will eventually get my funds back next week.Best to avoid.Now we know Friday maturity means MHBS keeps your funds over the weekend - convenient (for MHBS). So you lose two days interest on maturity.Maturity on Friday in my book says repayment on Friday. I can make same day electronic payments so why can't MHBS? Cynical response, so you lose two days interest.So MHBS assert the maturity date is not exactly the maturity date, Convenient (for MHBS).Excuses, excuses -free money for MHBS over the weekend - Great if you can get it at zero interest.Strangely even the current offerings mature on a Friday (What a coincidence!). YOU LOSE THE INTEREST TO WHICH YOU ARE ENTITLED!Last B/S I will invest with in the future. I have not seen this cynical attitude with any of the many deposit takers I deal with.

reviews.title: Lorraine,
reviews.body: Lorraine,Was polite, listen to what we both wanted to say, very understanding of our concern at not able to do on-line banking.Lorraine, was kind, supportive, professional and knowledgeable, and we left the building Society with confidence in what we achieved

reviews.title: Superb service at Market Harborough Building Society
reviews.body: Thank you so much to Hannah at Market Harborough building society.She was friendly, efficient and could not have been more helpful- it was lovely to meet someone so approachable rather than try to navigate the details of a savings account on a computer screen.Thelma

reviews.title: Elvira's excellent service
reviews.body: Elvira at your Kettering branch gave us all the information we needed and answered all our questions.She very efficiently opened our new accounts. Overall we were very impressed with her attention to detail and friendly manner.

reviews.title: A lovely  Building  society
reviews.body: Recently  I went to Market Harborough  Building  society  and opened a new savings account,  they are such a lovely group of very caring and helpful people.The lady I saw was Hannah Sunny such a lovely helpful  and caring person thanks  very much Hannah for all your help it made my day. X

reviews.title: Very efficient account opening
reviews.body: Very efficient account opening service from Claire.

reviews.title: Superb customer service
reviews.body: Went in to open three different accounts. Despite being short-staffed they accommodated me without an appointment there and then. Very professional, very polite, nice cup of tea, all done efficiently and with a smile. I dealt with Claire Reedman who was amazing. Very strongly recommend.

reviews.title: New savings account opening
reviews.body: Visited the branch in Market Harborough as the account could only be opened in branch. Out of the way for me but they were extremely efficient. Three staff serving, and the lady (Claire) opened the account even though all three staff were busy at the counter. As existing customer she found my details. She asked if I had read how the account works online (I had) and provided the new account number and explained how to set up the standing order. I already had a regular saver with them, she understood that I didn't need everything explained. Very good perception of what the customer needs. Receipt given, no need for  passbook as withdrawals can't be made, and a copy of the key facts given.A very good example of how to deal with a customer in a busy place. Well done to MHBS.I did notice they had a machine inside that was used by others for deposits etc, freeing up time for cashiers. I noticed people were also able to deposit coins.

reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: The product they were offering had one of the best interest rates available and suited my requirements

reviews.title: Great customer service
reviews.body: I have required assistance from the customer support team at Market Harborough on a couple of occasions in the last few months over my savings account.  On both occasions I received prompt, courteous and excellent customer service - in particular from Amelia.  I am glad to be a member of Market Harborough Building Society.

reviews.title: Excellent and professional service…
reviews.body: Excellent and professional service opening an account this morning from Jenni, also Keely and Leigh always friendly and helpful at the Desborough branch.

reviews.title: Opening new account
reviews.body: We were fealty with in an efficient and friendly manner.  Given a drink whilst we were being dealt with.

reviews.title: You made everything so easy to follow!
reviews.body: You made everything so straight forward and easy online to open an account with you. Everything completed quickly and efficiently which is rare nowadays!

reviews.title: First class service from Claire Reedman…
reviews.body: First class service from Claire Reedman at MHBS this morning.  Despite being short staffed she went out of the way to answer my queries and to open a new account for me.  Professional, friendly and entirely competent, thank you.

reviews.title: Excellent company
reviews.body: Excellent company. No issues at all. Friendly service. Very helpful. Been with this company for 42 years

reviews.title: Always a professional and friendly…
reviews.body: Always a professional and friendly service from Keely and Leigh at the Desborough branch.

reviews.title: Brilliant service lady on front desk…
reviews.body: Brilliant service lady on front desk explained in full about my next 2 year plan and set it all up for me

reviews.title: Visit to local branch
reviews.body: I visited my local branch and it was a good experience. The lady behind the counter was friendly and very helpful when I asked about my accounts.

reviews.title: we had a review of our accounts
reviews.body: they gave us the imformation we needed and did the changes necessary

reviews.title: Money transfer
reviews.body: My message was dealt with promptly and efficiently with great communication

reviews.title: Excellent service
reviews.body: I went into the Market Harborough branch to deposit money in my regular saver, the service was first class as always.After I had completed the transaction I was made aware of a new offering enabling me to open another account,given that the interest rate was very competitive I jumped at the opportunity.I was served by Claire Reedman who was very knowledgeable and processed my application in a friendly and professional manner.

reviews.title: Exceptional Service and got straight…
reviews.body: Exceptional Service and got straight through on the phone to a real person.

reviews.title: Staff were friendly
reviews.body: Staff were friendly, helpful and approachable.

reviews.title: Meeting with Jay
reviews.body: Meeting with Jay, she was lovely , polite and friendly. Did everything for us that we requested very efficiently.

reviews.title: As always kind
reviews.body: As always kind, patient and professional

reviews.title: Transfer of funds
reviews.body: The Transfer was made quite quickly and I understood in this account it would take 30 days.  No problem to me that's why I came in over a month before I needed that money.

reviews.title: Easy transfers
reviews.body: It was easy to transfer Isas and the service was excellent. Cash withdrawal was in my bank account the next day and the new isa information quickly followed.

reviews.title: Personal Service
reviews.body: I like the personal service given in your Market Harborough branch. The staff are always cheerful, friendly and efficient. They answer any questions I may have.  I do not want to deal with my account on line.Personal service is invaluable.

reviews.title: Poor interest rate for long term…
reviews.body: Poor interest rate for long term accounts , why keep reducing it when the Bank of England isn’t reducing their rates

reviews.title: Friendly branch open on Saturdays (and week days)

reviews.title: Excellent and simple to register.

reviews.title: New Account
reviews.body: Rachel was helpful, professional and friendly when dealing with my queries. Everything was fully explained and the whole process was straightforward.

reviews.title: Happy investorfound all your staff at the Market…
reviews.body: I found all your staff at the Market Harborough office to be very helpful, patient  efficient and professional. This is the second time I have trusted my money with your building society. Thank you.

reviews.title: Opening a savings account
reviews.body: Andy made the whole process very straightforward. He spent time to answer my assorted questions and explained the process really well. He was both friendly and professional. He is an asset to the company.

reviews.title: Helpful counter assistant who qui key…
reviews.body: Helpful counter assistant who qui key dealt with a very small transfer of just £20 between accounts in a friendly way.

reviews.title: Good management
reviews.body: Toby the manager is very good at his job easy to talk too and gets to know his customers.    Bill
